story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% and retained its "neutral" policy stance during the June 2026 meeting. The RBI also revised its GDP growth forecast for the year to 6.6% from 6.9% and announced measures to boost foreign exchange inflows and reserves.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is considering tax relief on government securities for foreign institutional investors (FIIs) to manage the rupee, as capital outflows and rising oil prices weigh on the currency. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das has stated the central bank will take necessary steps to ensure orderly rupee movements. Analysts suggest these measures may provide the RBI with flexibility to maintain its current policy stance.

story_clusters.summary: Vladimir Putin has emphasized the strength of India-Russia relations, stating that U.S. pressure will not affect their partnership and calling New Delhi a "reliable partner." He also rejected claims that Russia is benefiting from increased oil sales amid the Iran conflict, asserting that Russia's goal is to end the war, while expressing openness to jointly developing fifth-generation fighter technology, including the Su-57, with India.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has denied reports of selling gold, stating that its gold holdings remain unchanged at 880.52 tonnes. Meanwhile, gold prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) fell below ₹1.59 lakh per 10 grams, and silver prices declined by 2% ahead of the RBI's policy decision.

articles.title: SC Disposes Petition Challenging Section 16(2)(c) of GST Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a petition challenging Section 16(2)(c) of the GST law but kept the constitutional challenge to this provision alive. The court directed the taxpayer to use existing appellate remedies instead of bypassing them. This leaves unresolved the controversy around Input Tax Credit (ITC) under the GST framework, which affects tax compliance and revenue collection.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court refused to bypass the statutory GST appeal mechanism and directed the taxpayer to pursue appellate remedies. However, it preserved the constitutional challenge to Section 16(2)(c), leaving the ITC controversy unresolved. The post SC Disposes Petition Challenging Section 16(2)(c) of GST Law appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Typographical Error in SCN Not Fatal, But Taxpayer Entitled to Fresh Adjudication
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a typographical error in a tax notice (DRC-01) did not invalidate the proceedings, as the relevant invoices were clearly identified. However, the matter was sent back for fresh adjudication, with the taxpayer required to deposit 10% of the disputed tax and file a reply. The decision highlights the balance between procedural errors and substantive justice in tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a typographical error in the tax period mentioned in the DRC-01 notice did not invalidate the proceedings because the relevant invoices were clearly identified. However, the matter was remanded for fresh adjudication subject to deposit of 10% of the disputed tax and filing of a reply. The post Typographical Error in SCN Not Fatal, But Taxpayer Entitled to Fresh Adjudication appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Remands Section 54EC Claim as Bond Availability Issue Required Verification
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has sent back a taxpayer's claim for exemption under Section 54EC of the Income Tax Act to the Assessing Officer for further verification. The appellate authority had initially rejected the claim on technical grounds, but ITAT ruled that the assessee's explanation regarding bond availability required proper examination. The case now requires the Assessing Officer to re-examine the claim. This ruling highlights the importance of procedural compliance in ta
articles.raw_summary: The appellate authority had rejected the exemption claim on limitation grounds, but ITAT held that the assessee’s explanation regarding bond availability warranted verification. The case was sent back to the Assessing Officer. The post ITAT Remands Section 54EC Claim as Bond Availability Issue Required Verification appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CAM successfully represents lenders before NCLAT; Contempt order against lenders set aside in Jyoti Structures matter
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) overturned a contempt order against lenders in the Jyoti Structures case, citing procedural violations by the NCLT. The ruling highlights the importance of mandatory safeguards in contempt proceedings. The NCLAT also issued fact-specific directions in the matter. The decision impacts how lenders and financial institutions may be held accountable in similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The NCLAT held that the NCLT had failed to comply with the mandatory procedural safeguards applicable to contempt proceedings and, therefore, the impugned order could not be sustained. While setting aside the contempt order, the NCLAT also granted certain fact-specific directions in the matter. The post CAM successfully represents lenders before NCLAT; Contempt order against lenders set aside in Jyoti Structures matter appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: CBIC Bars Direct DGFT References to Ensure Uniform FTP Interpretation
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has instructed Customs officials not to seek clarifications directly from the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) regarding the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP). The new mechanism aims to ensure consistent interpretation and faster decision-making in trade policy implementation. This directive is intended to streamline trade policy enforcement and reduce inconsistencies in its application.

articles.title: No Addition for Notional Interest Based Solely on Seized Working Sheet: ITAT Mumba
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Mumbai ruled that the tax department cannot add notional interest income to a taxpayer's assessment based solely on presumptions or notings in seized working sheets. The tribunal emphasized that proof of actual receipt of interest income is required to sustain such additions. This decision underscores the need for concrete evidence in tax assessments rather than relying on assumptions from seized documents.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that no addition could be sustained where the tax department failed to establish actual receipt of interest income. The key takeaway is that presumptions and notings in seized documents cannot substitute proof of income. The post No Addition for Notional Interest Based Solely on Seized Working Sheet: ITAT Mumba appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST Refunds Section 54(3): Export under LUT & Inverted Duty Structure
articles.summary: The article explains Section 54(3) of India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) law, which allows refunds of unutilized Input Tax Credit (ITC) for zero-rated exports and cases of inverted duty structures. It emphasizes that refund entitlements are derived from the statute itself and cannot be restricted beyond the exclusions prescribed by law. The analysis is based on GST refund mechanisms under the LUT (Letter of Undertaking) framework.
articles.raw_summary: Section 54(3) permits refund of unutilised ITC for zero-rated supplies and inverted duty structures. The key takeaway is that refund entitlement flows from the statute and cannot be curtailed beyond prescribed exclusions. articles.title: Imagining International AI Governance In 'An Anarchic World Order'
articles.summary: The article explores the paradoxical concept of an 'anarchic world order' to describe the current state of international affairs, highlighting the lack of structured global governance. It reflects on the transition from the Cold War and unipolarity to a more chaotic geopolitical landscape. The piece does not focus on India-specific policies or actions but rather on broader international dynamics.

Then it could mean individual or collective attempts to sabotage the order is prevalent. There is no other better nomenclature to articulate present state of international affairs. For around forty-five years present world order had seen off the cold war, almost a few decades of unipolarity. Current state of international affairs can arguably be term

articles.title: Why the RBI kept rates unchanged amid oil, rupee and geopolitical pressures
articles.summary: RBI Repo Rate: The Reserve Bank of India has maintained its key interest rate at 5.25%. This decision prioritizes economic growth as inflation remains below the central bank's target. Despite rising oil prices and a weaker rupee, consumer price pressures are contained. Policymakers are monitoring global developments and will assess the impact of higher energy costs before considering further action.

articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court closed a writ petition filed by Cheq Digital after the Directorate General of GST Intelligence (DGGI) withdrew its provisional attachment of three of the company's bank accounts. The DGGI had attached the accounts under Form GST DRC-22, but later revoked the order during the court proceedings. ICICI Bank confirmed it would immediately implement the withdrawal. The case highlights procedural aspects of tax enforcement and corporate relief mechanisms.
articles.raw_summary: Cheq Digital's writ petition challenging DGGI's provisional attachment of its bank accounts under Form GST DRC-22 was disposed of as infructuous after DGGI itself withdrew the attachment order during pendency, with ICICI Bank undertaking to implement the withdrawal forthwith. The post Karnataka HC Disposes of Writ Petition as Infructuous After DGGI Withdraws Provisional Attachment of Three Bank Accounts appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: The Emerging Crime of Ecocide: How International Law is Evolving to Protect the Planet
articles.summary: The article explores the emerging legal concept of ecocide—defined as mass environmental destruction—as a potential crime under international law. Author Vansh Arora and editor Dr. Linda S. Spedding discuss how this evolving framework could strengthen global environmental protection efforts. The piece highlights the intersection of legal developments and policy shifts, particularly in the context of India's engagement with international environmental agreements and domestic regulatory frameworks

articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has amended rules to allow companies to use a portion of their Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds for Zero Coupon Zero Principal (ZCZP) instruments listed on the Social Stock Exchange. This creates a new regulated channel for deploying CSR funds, aligning corporate social impact with financial instruments. The move aims to enhance transparency and efficiency in CSR spending while leveraging market mechanisms for social good.

The Court further held that the petitioner had an efficacious statutory remedy of revision before the Special Tribunal

articles.title: IBBI Notifies CIRP Amendment Regulations 2026 to Reduce Insolvency Disputes
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has notified amendments to the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) Regulations for 2026. The changes aim to reduce insolvency disputes by enhancing disclosures, improving access to information, and introducing time-bound communication for claim settlements. These amendments seek to make the insolvency resolution process more transparent and resilient to disputes.
articles.raw_summary: IBBI has amended the CIRP Regulations to enhance disclosures, strengthen information access, and introduce time-bound claim communication. The changes aim to make insolvency resolution more transparent and dispute-resilient. The post IBBI Notifies CIRP Amendment Regulations 2026 to Reduce Insolvency Disputes appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, boosts rupee with new measures
articles.summary: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% and maintained a neutral stance, citing amplified inflation risks. To bolster the rupee, the central bank introduced multiple measures, including expanding accessible securities for foreign investors and increasing investment limits for NRIs. These actions aim to attract foreign capital and strengthen India's balance of payments.

articles.title: IBBI Amends Pre-Pack Regulations as Statutory Forms Are Replaced With Board-Notified Formats
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has amended pre-pack insolvency regulations by replacing existing statutory forms (P1–P14) with formats to be notified by the board itself. The prescribed forms in the Schedule have also been removed. This change streamlines regulatory compliance by allowing the IBBI to update formats as needed without requiring formal amendments.
articles.raw_summary: The Third Amendment Regulations, 2026 replace references to Forms P1–P14 with formats to be notified by the IBBI. The Schedule containing the prescribed forms has also been omitted. The post IBBI Amends Pre-Pack Regulations as Statutory Forms Are Replaced With Board-Notified Formats appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: E-Way Bill Dilemma: Penalty for Internal Branch & Job Site Transfers Under GST?
articles.summary: A ruling examines whether penalties under Section 129 of the GST Act can be imposed for internal movement of goods between branches or job sites without an e-way bill. The case highlights that where no tax is payable, the statutory penalty formula may not apply. The ruling has implications for businesses handling inter-state or intra-state transfers within the same entity. TaxGuru reports on this legal interpretation affecting GST compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling examines whether penalties under Section 129 can be imposed for internal movement of goods without an e-way bill. It highlights that where no tax is payable, the statutory penalty formula may fail. This exemption aims to attract more foreign investment into India's government securities market.

articles.title: IBBI mandates CoC approval for asset sales, valuations, legal proceedings, liquidator fees etc
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has mandated that the Committee of Creditors (CoC) must approve critical decisions during liquidation, including asset sales, valuations, legal proceedings, and liquidator fees. This change, effective from 2026 amendments, shifts decision-making authority from consultation to creditor oversight. The move aims to strengthen the role of creditors in the insolvency process. The IBBI is India's regulator for insolvency and bankruptcy matters.

The changes shift major decision-making authority from consultation mechanisms to creditor oversight. articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has notified amendments to several insolvency regulations to implement the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Act, 2026. These amendments aim to operationalize the updated legal framework for insolvency and bankruptcy in India.

articles.title: From Play to Pay GST: Decoding SC’s Verdict on Online Gaming
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that online money gaming involving stakes is a taxable supply under GST, requiring payment on the full value of player deposits rather than just platform commissions. The verdict clarifies that such gaming constitutes a supply of actionable claims. The decision impacts online gaming platforms and players across India, setting a precedent for GST compliance in the sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that online money gaming involving stakes constitutes a taxable supply of actionable claims. GST is payable on the full value of player deposits and not merely on platform commissions. The post From Play to Pay GST: Decoding SC’s Verdict on Online Gaming appeared first on TaxGuru .

The Tribunal deleted the disallowance after finding non-compliance with Section 14A(2). The post No 14A Disallowance Without Satisfaction, No Penalty on Debatable Issues appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: How to Avoid GST Notices on Proportionate ITC Reversal?
articles.summary: The article explains how businesses can avoid GST notices related to proportionate Input Tax Credit (ITC) reversal. It highlights the importance of complying with GST Rules 42 and 43, which govern the apportionment of ITC for common inputs. Non-compliance may result in demands, interest, and penalties from tax authorities. The guidance is aimed at businesses claiming common input tax credits under India's GST framework.

articles.title: Royalty Hike Under MMDR Act Overrides Contractual Terms  : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that royalty on minerals under the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957 must be paid at the rate prevailing on the date of dispatch or removal from the mine, overriding any contractual terms. The decision clarifies that if the dispatch occurs after a royalty hike, the higher rate applies regardless of prior agreements. This ruling impacts mining companies and the government's revenue from mineral royalties.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Thursday (June 4) held that royalty on minerals under the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957 must be paid at the rate prevailing on the date of their actual dispatch or removal from the mine, irrespective of the rate contemplated under the parties' contract. “…the payment is to be made on the date of the movement of the minerals. articles.title: DGFT Expands QCO Exemption for SEZ Imports to All Authorized Operational Goods
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has expanded exemptions for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units and developers, allowing imports of all permissible goods required for authorized operations. Compliance with Quality Control Orders (QCO) and Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) norms will only apply when goods are cleared into the Domestic Tariff Area. articles.title: IBBI (Inspection and Investigation) (Amendment) Regulations, 2026
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) amended its Inspection and Investigation Regulations in 2026. The amendment replaces references to whole-time members with a flexible framework allowing the Disciplinary Committee to include one or more persons. This update modernizes the regulatory structure governing inspections and investigations under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The change aims to streamline disciplinary processes within the IBBI.

The changes update the regulatory structure governing inspections and investigations under the IBC framework. articles.title: SC Keeps Section GST ITC Section 16(2)(c) Challenge Open: Impact on Taxpayers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has left open a constitutional challenge to Section 16(2)(c) of the GST law, which ties Input Tax Credit (ITC) eligibility to whether suppliers have paid their taxes. This means taxpayers who were denied ITC due to supplier defaults may still have a legal avenue to contest such denials. The ruling keeps the issue unresolved but provides potential relief for bona fide buyers.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has kept open the constitutional challenge to Section 16(2)(c), which links ITC eligibility to supplier tax payment. The key takeaway is that bona fide buyers may still have a chance to challenge ITC denial based on supplier defaults. The post SC Keeps Section GST ITC Section 16(2)(c) Challenge Open: Impact on Taxpayers appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) has revised rules for personal guarantors undergoing bankruptcy or insolvency proceedings. The changes aim to strengthen oversight in corporate insolvency cases and protect creditor interests.

The CECA negotiations are the moment to say so.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu to end highway contract commission culture, says Minister Aadhav Arjuna
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's government, led by Minister Aadhav Arjuna, has announced major reforms to its highway contracting system. The changes include ending commission-based practices, implementing automated billing and faster payments, and barring benami companies from road projects. These measures aim to improve transparency, attract more infrastructure investment, and speed up the execution of road projects across the state.

articles.title: SC Revives FIR: “Do Something for These Boys” Viewed as Prima Facie Bribe Solicitation
articles.summary: The Supreme Court revived an FIR against public officials, ruling that Section 7 of the Prevention of Corruption Act covers attempts to obtain undue advantage through intermediaries. The court held that officials cannot avoid liability by using subordinates or third parties to solicit bribes. The phrase 'Do Something for These Boys' was deemed prima facie evidence of bribe solicitation. This decision reinforces anti-corruption enforcement and clarifies legal liability for officials.

The proposal aims to address cost and supply challenges in road construction while promoting sustainable alternatives.

Officials are exploring new funding methods for dollar support operations. Investors are closely monitoring potential policy moves by Japan.

Investors watch for further policy moves.

articles.title: Banning Online Money Gaming is within States’ Power: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that online money gaming, where stakes are placed on uncertain outcomes, constitutes betting and gambling, regardless of whether skill or chance is involved. This empowers state governments to prohibit such activities. The judgment clarifies the legal framework for regulating online gaming platforms in India. The decision was delivered by the Supreme Court of India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court ruled that once money is staked on an uncertain outcome, the activity becomes betting and gambling regardless of whether the game involves skill or chance. States therefore have the power to prohibit online gaming played for stakes. The post Banning Online Money Gaming is within States’ Power: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: Global economic growth faces risks from stagflation as rising oil prices, fueled by geopolitical tensions like those involving Iran, threaten to push inflation higher. Experts warn central banks may maintain high interest rates longer than expected, potentially destabilizing economies worldwide. The situation could impact India's economic outlook, influencing policy decisions on inflation and energy security.
articles.raw_summary: Global economic outlook darkens with stagflation fears as energy prices fuel persistent inflation. Experts warn central banks may keep interest rates high longer than anticipated. Geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning Iran, are prolonging market uncertainty and could push oil prices back into triple digits, impacting global economic stability.

articles.title: Bogus Supplier Tag Alone Not Enough: ITAT Deletes Purchase and Loan Additions
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that labeling a supplier as 'bogus' is insufficient evidence to justify tax additions against an assessee. The tribunal emphasized that tax authorities must provide independent proof of the assessee's own transactions to sustain additions. This decision impacts how tax investigations are conducted and sets a precedent for future cases involving supplier-related tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that an investigation report against a supplier is only a starting point for inquiry and not conclusive proof against the assessee. The key takeaway is that additions require independent evidence relating to the assessee’s own transactions The post Bogus Supplier Tag Alone Not Enough: ITAT Deletes Purchase and Loan Additions appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Share Buyback Taxation Comes Full Circle with Return to Capital Gains Model
articles.summary: The Indian government will revert to taxing share buyback proceeds as capital gains starting 1 April 2026. Doctors warn that prolonged unavailability of these essential medicines, with limited alternatives, could negatively impact patient outcomes and survival rates.

articles.title: Gambling – GST leviable irrespective of Game of Chance vs. Skill: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that any activity involving money staked on an uncertain outcome qualifies as betting or gambling for Goods and Services Tax (GST) purposes. The court clarified that GST is payable on the full bet value or deposit, not just platform fees or commissions. This decision impacts tax liability for gambling-related transactions in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court ruled that whenever money is staked on an uncertain outcome, the activity amounts to betting and gambling for GST purposes. GST is payable on the entire bet value or deposit and not merely on platform fees or commissions. The post Gambling – GST leviable irrespective of Game of Chance vs. Skill: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Supreme Court draws red lines for AI in courts in draft regulations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has proposed draft regulations governing the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in judicial proceedings. The regulations prohibit AI from being used for decision-making and require lawyers to disclose any AI assistance they use. This move aims to ensure transparency and accountability in the judicial process while addressing ethical concerns around AI in legal settings.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court proposes regulations on AI in courts, banning AI for decision-making, requiring disclosure of AI assistance by lawyers.

articles.title: SC’s draft rules allow AI in courts, bar it in decision-making
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has proposed draft rules allowing the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in courts for tasks like legal research and drafting, but explicitly barring AI from decision-making. Judges will retain sole authority over rulings, and lawyers must disclose any AI use in their filings. The framework aims to improve efficiency while preserving judicial independence and due process.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has proposed new rules for artificial intelligence in courts. These rules allow AI for tasks like legal research and drafting. However, AI will not be used to decide court cases. Judges will remain the sole decision-makers. Lawyers must disclose AI use in their filings. This framework aims to enhance efficiency while upholding judicial authority and due process.

articles.title: Form 26AS – Not Conclusive In Presumptive Taxation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has ruled that business income cannot be reclassified as professional income solely because services are rendered. This clarification impacts how presumptive taxation is applied under the Income Tax Act. The ruling emphasizes that statutory classification, not Form 26AS, determines tax treatment. The decision is significant for taxpayers and tax authorities navigating income classification disputes.
articles.raw_summary: Recent ITAT rulings clarify that presumptive taxation depends on statutory classification under the Income Tax Act. The key takeaway is that business income cannot be reclassified as professional income merely because services are rendered. articles.title: Do Sons and Daughters have Equal Rights in HUF Property?
articles.summary: The 2005 amendment to Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) laws granted daughters equal coparcenary rights, including inheritance and partition rights, similar to sons. However, their children do not automatically gain coparcenary status at birth, unlike sons. This legal change impacts family property rights and gender equality in inheritance laws. The article is published by TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The 2005 amendment granted daughters equal coparcenary rights in HUF property, including inheritance and partition rights. However, their children do not acquire coparcenary status by birth, creating a key distinction from sons. The post Do Sons and Daughters have Equal Rights in HUF Property? appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Can govt employee be terminated from service for suppressing higher qualification: SC to examine
articles.summary: Can a government employee be terminated from service for suppressing information about possessing higher qualifications than the prescribed degree for the job? The Supreme Court on Tuesday agreed to examine a Telangana High Court verdict, which upheld the Centre's decision to terminate the service of a Scheduled Tribe person who had allegedly suppressed the information of being 'intermediate pass' than the prescribed 'tenth pass' criteria required for the job of 'work assistant'.

articles.title: Bangladesh FM Khalilur Rahman elected president of 81st UNGA, Jaishankar congratulates counterpart
articles.summary: Bangladesh's Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman has been elected President of the 81st United Nations General Assembly (UNGA). Rahman, who took office as Bangladesh's foreign minister earlier this year, is the first Bangladeshi to hold this position since 1986-87. External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar congratulated Rahman on his election. The role may influence international diplomacy and regional dynamics, including India's engagements with the UN and Bangladesh.
articles.raw_summary: Bangladesh last had presidency of UNGA in the 41st (1986-87) session. Rahman was sworn in as Bangladesh’s foreign minister in February this year.

articles.title: From 215 to 20 in a month: The stunning collapse of Mamata's political empire
articles.summary: The article examines the rapid decline in Mamata Banerjee's political influence in West Bengal, dropping from 215 to 20 seats in a month. Observers suggest her ability to frame herself as a victim of political engineering could help her retain support among party workers. The focus is on whether she can turn adversity into an opportunity amid shifting political fortunes.

If she succeeds in portraying herself as a victim of political engineering, she may yet retain her stature among the party’s grassroots workers.

articles.title: Kerala launches statewide cyber safety pledge for students on school reopening day
articles.summary: Kerala Police launched the 'Kid Glove Project' on June 1, when schools reopened, requiring students to take a cyber safety pledge. The initiative aims to educate young internet users about online threats, cyber hygiene, privacy protection, fake news, and responsible digital behavior. The project is part of Kerala's efforts to address growing concerns about digital risks among schoolchildren.

The bot will also connect users with vendors, simplifying the process for beneficiaries. The scheme aims to promote solar energy adoption among households. The initiative is part of the government's renewable energy and welfare policies.

articles.title: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
articles.summary: China has been expanding its territorial claims in the South China Sea by creating artificial land. Other countries in the region are now adopting similar tactics to assert their own claims. This escalation raises concerns about maritime disputes, regional stability, and the potential for conflict. The situation could influence India's diplomatic and security policies in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.raw_summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

articles.title: What is El Niño and how could it affect weather this year?
articles.summary: A strong El Niño event, termed a 'super El Niño,' is expected this year, which could cause extreme weather patterns like heavy rainfall and worsening droughts. The phenomenon is a natural climate cycle that affects global weather systems. Such events often prompt governments to prepare for climate-related disasters and adjust policies for agriculture and water management.
articles.raw_summary: A strong El Niño this year, colloquially dubbed a super El Niño, could lead to extreme weather events — from heavy rainfall to exacerbated droughts.

articles.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
articles.summary: SSRIs like Prozac and Zoloft are widely prescribed antidepressants, but some experts argue they are overused. The article highlights concerns about careless prescribing practices and the need for better regulation. While not specific to India, the discussion on drug overprescription could influence global or domestic healthcare policies.
articles.raw_summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

articles.title: Constitution expert Subhash Kashyap dies at 97
articles.summary: Renowned constitutional law and parliamentary affairs expert Subhash C Kashyap passed away at 97. His extensive writings on Parliament made him a significant figure in political science and law.

articles.title: ‘Gaddi’ vs private property: The royal inheritance conundrum & how SC settled it with Kapurthala ruling
articles.summary: The Supreme Court settled a 50-year-old property dispute involving princely states like Dholpur, Rampur, and Kapurthala, clarifying the distinction between 'Gaddi' (royal inheritance) and private property. The ruling provides the clearest resolution yet on how royal properties should be inherited and managed. The case involved long-standing legal ambiguities over the rights of former royal families to their ancestral properties.
articles.raw_summary: From Dholpur   Rampur to Kapurthala, courts have grappled with matters of princely properties. Top court’s latest order in 50-year-old property dispute offers the clearest resolution yet.

The government's adjustment reflects concerns over potential agricultural impacts due to weaker monsoon rains.

articles.title: Parimatch probe takes ED to Zepto's doorstep

As part of the investigation, the agency questioned Zepto, a quick commerce app, about its promotional campaigns for the banned betting app. Investigators found Parimatch used Zepto's delivery flyers to reach households. This probe could lead to stricter regulations or enforcement against illegal betting operations in India.

The scenario assumes continued conflicts into 2027, affecting energy supplies and trade routes.

articles.title: By 2030, AI could consume 3% of world’s electricity, as much water as 1.3 billion people: UN report
articles.summary: A UN report warns that by 2030, AI infrastructure could consume 3% of the world's electricity and as much water as 1.3 billion people. The report highlights the growing strain on global resources due to AI's expansion. While not India-specific, the findings may prompt domestic discussions on AI governance, energy policies, and environmental sustainability.
articles.raw_summary: The report warns that the infrastructure powering AI could place unprecedented pressure on global electricity, water and land resources over the coming years.

articles.title: Rajesh Exports case shows why investors must look beyond profit and revenue growth: IIAS
articles.summary: Commenting on SEBI's interim order against Rajesh Exports, Hetal Dalal, President and COO of IIAS, highlighted concerns around audit committee composition, auditor independence and transparency. Dalal expects further regulatory scrutiny as the investigation progresses and urged investors to study annual reports and regulatory filings closely.

articles.title: Anthropic says AI labs need coordinated plan to halt development if risks rise
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has warned that AI systems capable of self-improvement could pose significant risks to human control over technology. The company suggests that AI labs need a coordinated plan to halt development if risks escalate. This raises concerns about the governance and regulation of advanced AI systems, which could have implications for global and domestic policy frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: AI that can build itself would be a major development in the history of technology, but "full recursive self-improvement also might increase the risks ‌of humans ⁠losing control ⁠over AI systems," the AI startup said.

articles.title: T-Mobile opens India tech centre, to hire nearly 1,000 by 2027
articles.summary: T-Mobile has launched its global capability center in Hyderabad, India. The tech center will focus on advanced software engineering, product development, and cybersecurity. T-Mobile plans to hire approximately 1,000 employees by 2027. Hyderabad was chosen for its strong technology talent and innovation ecosystem. This expansion highlights India's growing role in global tech operations.

articles.title: British lawmaker sues Musk's xAI over sexualised Grok images
articles.summary: A British lawmaker has filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk's AI company xAI, alleging that its Grok AI tool generated non-consensual sexualized images. Grok is distributed via Musk's social media platform X and is already under regulatory scrutiny in several countries for similar concerns. The case raises questions about AI ethics, user safety, and the accountability of tech platforms in preventing misuse of AI tools.
articles.raw_summary: Grok, distributed through Musk's social media platform X, is currently subject to regulatory probes in several countries after an outcry earlier this year over its use to create non-consensual sexualised images.

articles.title: Companies face hardware talent crunch amid AI boom
articles.summary: India faces a significant shortage of AI hardware engineers, including HVAC, robotics, and industrial automation specialists, as AI adoption surges. This demand, driven by smart manufacturing, EVs, and data centres, has led to a 35% salary increase for these roles. The AI boom now extends beyond software, impacting the entire infrastructure ecosystem.

articles.title: FDI equity inflows up 18% in FY26, US investments double
articles.summary: India saw a significant jump in foreign investment in the last fiscal year. FDI equity inflows increased by 18 percent, reaching $58.84 billion. The United States and Singapore were major contributors to this growth. Overall FDI, including reinvested earnings, also saw a substantial rise. Key sectors attracting investment included computer software and hardware, services, and trading.

Key sectors attracting investment included computer software and hardware, services, and trading.

articles.title: Govt sets up six sector groups to fast-track ‘Make in India 2.0’ push
articles.summary: In a bold move towards self-sufficiency, India is ramping up its domestic manufacturing initiatives! Six government-led teams are tasked with selecting 100 essential products that will help reduce reliance on imports. Target sectors include pharmaceuticals, textiles, and electronics. This strategic effort aims to boost local industry and mitigate the import expenditure.

articles.title: Livestock industry demands 1.5 million tonnes soymeal imports amid price surge
articles.summary: India's livestock sector faces rising feed costs. Farmers are urging the government to permit soybean meal imports. Domestic prices have surged significantly. This increase impacts poultry farmers and squeezes profit margins. The soybean processing industry opposes imports, citing local availability. This situation affects the entire livestock industry.

articles.title: Cold storages struggle as Gulf cargo lands in India
articles.summary: Indian cold storages are full of imported apples, avocados, and dry fruits. Shipping disruptions due to the Iran war are causing these diversions. This situation also impacts India's own produce exports. Traders note a slowdown in demand for dry fruits in secondary markets. Avocado imports faced shortages earlier due to shipping route closures.

Avocado imports faced shortages earlier due to shipping route closures.

articles.title: From inputs to retail, a monster rages through: As supply-side inflation pressures build, the implications for monetary policy
articles.summary: India's inflation is shifting from producers to consumers, driven by cost-push factors like rising global commodity and energy prices. This is evident in the widening gap between WPI and CPI inflation. Subsequent retail fuel price hikes are expected to further increase transportation costs, impacting consumer prices across various sectors.

articles.summary: India is boosting domestic manufacturing. Six expert groups will identify 100 products to reduce import dependence. The initiative highlights the growing importance of responsible AI innovation, which could influence future policy discussions in India regarding AI ethics and regulation.

articles.summary: India leads the world in using AI at work. Employees and managers are adopting AI tools widely. This adoption boosts job satisfaction significantly. articles.title: PhysicsWallah revises lending strategy, to partner with NBFCs for student loans
articles.summary: PhysicsWallah, an edtech company, has revised its lending strategy to partner with non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) for student loans instead of lending directly. The company will now act as a technology platform connecting students to regulated lending partners. This shift follows an earlier plan to lend through its books with FinZ infusion. The move highlights the growing role of fintech in education financing.
articles.raw_summary: In a filing on June 4, the company said it will now tie up with third-party non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) instead of lending through its books, as had been envisaged with the FinZ infusion. The edtech will henceforth work as a technology platform that connects its students to a curated list of regulated lending partners.

articles.title: Top AI leaders, including Sam Altman, warn AI could make dangerous biological knowledge easier to access
articles.summary: Top AI leaders, including Sam Altman, have warned that artificial intelligence could make dangerous biological knowledge more accessible. They urged mandatory screening and recordkeeping for synthetic DNA purchases to mitigate risks. The group emphasized the need for safeguards as AI lowers barriers to advanced biological research. This issue could have implications for India's biosecurity and technology governance policies.
articles.raw_summary: The group warned that AI could lower barriers to advanced biological knowledge and urged mandatory screening and recordkeeping for synthetic DNA purchases.

articles.title: Thynk Technology closure: CEO, HR arrested after Pune IT firm shuts overnight, 700 employees jobless
articles.summary: Thynk Technology, a Pune-based IT firm, abruptly shut down overnight, leaving around 700 employees jobless. Many recruits had completed training but were awaiting project assignments and job confirmations. The CEO and HR head were arrested in connection with the closure. The incident raises concerns about labour rights, corporate accountability, and potential regulatory gaps in protecting workers during sudden company failures.
articles.raw_summary: Many recruits completed the training and waited for project assignments and job confirmations.

articles.title: Heat and tomatoes are burning a hole in India’s thalis
articles.summary: The cost of cooking a meal at home in India climbed to its highest level in three months in May, with a non-vegetarian thali rising to ₹56.5, its highest since December 2025, and a vegetarian thali hitting ₹27.4. Tomato prices surging 57% on-year and global supply disruptions in vegetable oil and LPG were the primary drivers.

articles.title: Malviya Nagar fire incident: MEA extends assistance, in touch with embassies
articles.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a massive fire engulfed a B B hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday. Firefighters rescued 37 occupants, while 39 victims were taken to Max Hospital, where 18 were declared dead and 15 remain in intensive care. Authorities said the hotel lacked a mandatory fire NOC, and police have launched a manhunt for the owner while investigating possible safety violations. Foreign nationals accounted for 17 of the 21 fatalities. Prime Mini

articles.title: Tigress Zeenat gives birth to 4 cubs in Odisha's Similipal, boosting tiger conservation
articles.summary: Tigress Zeenat, relocated from Maharashtra's Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha's Similipal Tiger Reserve, has given birth to four healthy cubs. Conservationists note that the cubs' high genetic diversity could strengthen Similipal's tiger population. This event is seen as a boost to India's tiger conservation efforts under existing wildlife protection policies.
articles.raw_summary: In a major boost to tiger conservation efforts, tigress Zeenat—translocated from Maharashtra's Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha's Similipal Tiger Reserve—has given birth to four healthy cubs. Conservationists say the cubs' high genetic diversity represents a significant step toward strengthening Similipal's tiger population and securing the future of the reserve's big cats.

articles.title: Srinagar airport to be shut for fortnight from Oct 1 for maintenance; locals fear blow to business
articles.summary: Srinagar airport will be closed for two weeks starting October 1 for maintenance. Local travel operators in Srinagar fear this closure will disrupt tourism, particularly from West Bengal, leading to cancellations and financial losses for businesses dependent on the seasonal influx of visitors.
articles.raw_summary: The Valley traditionally witnesses a massive influx of tourists from West Bengal during this period, and travel operators fear the closure will lead to widespread cancellations and heavy financial losses.

articles.title: Maharashtra to waive recovery from up to 30 lakh ineligible Ladki Bahin beneficiaries; ₹5,000 crore at stake

articles.summary: Thousands of dead fish washed ashore in a creek in Maharashtra's Palghar district. Officials suspect extreme heat and possible industrial pollution as causes and have collected samples for investigation. The incident highlights concerns over environmental degradation and regulatory gaps in pollution control.
articles.raw_summary: Thousands of dead fish washed up in Maharashtra's Palghar district, raising concerns over extreme heat and possible industrial pollution. Officials have collected samples to determine the exact cause.

articles.title: Gaganyaan Mission: Shubhanshu Shukla says human spaceflight is a big leap for India
articles.summary: A year after becoming the first Indian to visit the International Space Station, astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla is preparing for ISRO's Gaganyaan mission. Drawing on his experience as an Indian Air Force test pilot, Shukla says India's first human spaceflight programme will be a transformative milestone that strengthens the country's position in global space exploration.

articles.title: WATCH: Badrinath pilgrims stranded for hours as massive traffic jam hits NH-7 near Joshimath
articles.summary: Thousands of pilgrims traveling to Badrinath were stranded for hours due to a massive traffic jam on NH-7 near Joshimath. The incident underscores challenges in managing high-traffic pilgrimage routes.

articles.title: 60k people pose hurdle for SIR in Manipur: How will EOs verify displaced voters?
articles.summary: The Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) of Manipur has raised concerns with the Election Commission of India (ECI) about verifying the identities of 60,000 displaced voters ahead of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process. The challenge stems from the displacement of people due to ongoing conflicts in the state, complicating voter registration and verification. The ECI is expected to address these logistical and administrative hurdles to ensure fair electoral processes.

articles.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it has been unable to inspect Iranian facilities, enriched uranium stockpile
articles.summary: The UN nuclear watchdog (IAEA) reports it has not been able to inspect most Iranian nuclear facilities since February, with only the Bushehr nuclear power plant visited in June. This raises concerns about Iran's enriched uranium stockpile and compliance with international nuclear agreements. The situation could impact global non-proliferation efforts and regional stability, which are relevant to India's diplomatic and energy policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The only nuclear facility inspected in Iran by IAEA since the last report in February has been the Bushehr nuclear power plant, which was visited on June 1-3.

The conflict highlights broader issues of interstate cooperation and governance in India's federal structure.

articles.summary: Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Iraq are investing heavily in alternative oil transport infrastructure like pipelines, rail, and storage facilities. articles.title: ‘No Bhagwan songs, don’t go to temple’: Woman says she was shown Pakistani preacher's videos in TCS Nashik case
articles.summary: A woman in Nashik alleged that a married man sexually exploited her under the pretext of marriage while pressuring her to abandon Hindu religious practices. The accused showed her videos of a Pakistani preacher during their interactions at a TCS office in Nashik. The incident highlights concerns about workplace safety and gender-based violence. The article does not cite any official policy response or legal action at this stage.
articles.raw_summary: The woman alleged that a married man sexually exploited her on the promise of marriage while encouraging her to move away from Hindu religious practices.

articles.title: 3 dead, worker's leg crushed, muddy site: What we know so far about Faridabad's deadly crane collapse
articles.summary: A crane collapsed at a construction site in Faridabad, crushing a shipping container used as a temporary office and resting area for workers. Three people died, and a worker suffered a crushed leg. The incident raises concerns about construction site safety and worker welfare in India. Officials have not yet commented on potential policy implications.
articles.raw_summary: The crane came crashing down onto a shipping container being used as a temporary site office and resting area for workers, officials said.

articles.title: Man flees after allegedly strangling wife to death in Gurugram; manhunt underway
articles.summary: A man in Gurugram allegedly strangled his wife to death after an altercation over her refusal to disclose who she was speaking to on the phone. Police have launched a manhunt for the suspect. The incident underscores concerns about domestic violence and women's safety in India, raising questions about law enforcement and societal responses to such crimes.
articles.raw_summary: The altercation ensued after the husband demanded to know who his wife was speaking to over the phone, information that she refused to reveal

articles.title: Pakistan Buys Priciest LNG Shipment in Years on Hormuz Closure
articles.summary: Pakistan has purchased its most expensive liquefied natural gas (LNG) shipment in years due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route blocked by Iran and the U.S. This crisis affects about a fifth of global LNG supply, raising concerns about energy security in the region. The situation highlights vulnerabilities in energy trade routes that could have broader implications for neighboring countries like India.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan is among the hardest-hit by the conflict in the Middle East, as about a fifth of global LNG supply remains stuck behind the narrow waterway blockaded by Iran and the US.
